read: Price pulled back after breaking 740; 741 named call wall caps any final-hour bounce, 735 is the mechanical floor if selling extends below 737.

levels.why: Largest charm node in the visible range (-56,244M) stacked with vanna (+1,504M) in a sharply rising-IV tape — dealers mechanically forced to sell into this strike into the close; gamma is bleeding (-534M) but charm and vanna fully dominate and intensify from here.

Named call wall (next above broken 740) with rapidly building GEX (+69.6M delta) and charm -4,280M — the most likely level tested in the final 35 min, but today's demonstrated willingness to break 740 means momentum can overshoot before the 740 GEX gravity pulls price back, so reaction is not guaranteed to be tick-clean.

Largest gamma wall below spot (536.7M, building +55.5M) with positive charm (+13,290M) creating a concentrated mechanical dealer buy zone into the close — primary floor on any downside extension through 737, and the concentrated greek signature favors a sharp to-the-tick hold.

Dominant combined OI (100k) and massive delta mass ($2.7B dex) define this as the structural floor, but 9 points from spot with positive GEX pinning into close sharply limits reach probability — strong conditional reversal, low fill odds today.

Reversed today but only after a 0.47pt grind — today's tape confirms this is a weak hold, not a tick-clean wall; a re-test in the final 35 minutes would likely produce the same choppy behavior rather than a clean limit-order entry.
